subject: Sociology
title: Global Pandemics
description: Learn Global Pandemics in one minute.

hook.text: COVID-19 killed millions. But it didn't kill equally. Class, race, and nation determined who survived.

buildup.text: Pandemics expose and deepen existing inequalities in healthcare, housing, employment, and social protection.

discovery.text: Essential workers — often low-paid and from minority groups — faced the highest exposure and the least protection.

twist.text: Vaccine nationalism meant wealthy nations secured doses first. Global cooperation collapsed when it mattered most.

climax.text: Pandemics don't create inequality. They reveal it — stripping away the illusion that everyone faces the same risks.

punchline.text: The virus was equal; the response wasn't.

quiz.question: How did COVID-19 reveal social inequality?
options: Class, race, and nation determined exposure, treatment, and survival
